$comment: Intent ledger — the deterministic 'redesign vs bug' source of truth for the parity closed loop. 'redesign vs bug' cannot be invented by a script; it needs the design intent (docs/ + owner ruling). So each such decision is made ONCE (by ui-parity-checker / owner) and RECORDED here, after which the loop classifies that divergence deterministically forever. A finding (structural-missing node, or a node-level divergence) that MATCHES an entry is 'redesign' (intended); anything not listed is 'BUG?' to investigate. Match: screen == entry.screen AND (entry.node == '*' OR node name startsWith entry.node) AND (entry.kind == '*' OR entry.kind == finding kind). source MUST cite a doc / memory / owner ruling.

intentional.reason: FE was redesigned to a quiet, low-accent surface; kit-02 is the pre-redesign engagement layout (more accent fills). Colour/quiet divergence on dashboard accent surfaces is intended, not a token bug.
